name: Quickbase No-Code Platform API
what_it_does: No-code operational platform with REST API — enabling operations and IT teams to build custom database applications, automate multi-step workflows, connect to external systems, and create dashboards for process management, project tracking, and operational data without coding.
use_cases: Reading and writing operational records in Quickbase tables via REST API
Automating multi-step workflows triggered by record changes or external events
Integrating Quickbase operations data with ERP and CRM systems
Building custom business intelligence reports from Quickbase operational data
Managing Quickbase application configuration and access programmatically
not_for: Simple spreadsheet replacement without workflow (use Airtable for lighter needs)
Enterprise ERP with built-in financial modules (use SAP or NetSuite)
Developer-heavy custom applications (use full-stack frameworks for complex apps)
best_when: An operations team in construction, manufacturing, or professional services needs to build and maintain complex operational workflows, project tracking, and resource management applications without heavy engineering investment.
avoid_when: You need simple data collection, enterprise ERP, complex UI applications, or developer-intensive custom features beyond operational database and workflow.

auth.notes: User token from Quickbase account. QB-USER-TOKEN header. Realm-scoped (Quickbase uses 'realm' for org). Webhook for record events. No OAuth2. Pipelines (Quickbase's built-in integration tool) connects to external systems. XML-based legacy API also available but REST API preferred.

pricing.notes: Massachusetts-based company (Boston). Originally part of Intuit (1999-2019). Spun out as independent company. Raised $350M+. Backed by Vista Equity Partners. 6,000+ companies including 70% of Fortune 100 use Quickbase. Dominant in construction, professional services, and manufacturing sectors. Operational platform differentiator vs productivity-focused no-code tools. Strong in field-to-office workflow automation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for records, tables, apps, reports, and users. QB-USER-TOKEN header. Good API documentation. Quickbase 'realm' is org-level scope. Webhook for record events. Pipelines for complex integrations configured in admin. XML legacy API still available but deprecated.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record reads fully idempotent. Table reads idempotent. Report reads idempotent. Record upsert supported via merge field — use for idempotent writes.
known_agent_gotchas: QB-USER-TOKEN header — not Authorization header, non-standard
Quickbase 'realm' is org-level scoping concept — required in API base URL
Offset pagination for records — manage large result set pagination carefully
XML legacy API still in use at some customers — verify which API version to use
Field IDs not field names in API — map field names to IDs before operations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, FedRAMP compliant. US data residency. QB-USER-TOKEN auth. Operational data (project, resource, field data) can include sensitive business and employee PII. FedRAMP authorization for US government deployments. Vista Equity Partners ownership brings enterprise security standards.
